steps.title: Identify the main clause structure
steps.content:
The key signal is that “reflected” is the only verb form that functions as the sentence's main predicate.

This reveals that “These extensive changes” and “reflected” are the core subject-verb pair, with the listed items—addition, removal, insertion—serving as explanatory content between them.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule
steps.content:
When a phrase interrupts the main clause between subject and verb, it must be set off by commas on both sides. “Changes, including the addition..., reflected” uses commas to clearly mark the interruption, keeping “reflected” as the sentence's main verb.

A) introduces a second main verb that conflicts with “reflected,” B) fragments the sentence with a bare verb, and C) makes “included” rather than “reflected” the main verb.

That confirms D is correct.
